assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions testing upgrade on dev clusters first and references the user's setup with dev on Rapid and prod on Stable channels

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: While it mentions Autopilot handles node upgrades automatically and no manual node pool management, it doesn't explicitly address mandatory resource requests or SSH limitations

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Mentions checking deprecated APIs and APIs removed in 1.32, but doesn't provide specific kubectl commands to perform these checks

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Does not specifically mention mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot requirement or concern

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Does not specifically mention Pod Disruption Budgets, bare pods, or graceful shutdown configurations in the pre-upgrade checklist

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Post-upgrade checklist mentions verifying cluster status and API server response but doesn't provide specific kubectl version verification commands

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Includes comprehensive workload validation items like pod status, service discovery, load balancer health, and application performance checks

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends testing upgrade on dev clusters first and suggests switching one dev cluster to Stable temporarily if needed

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Does not mention the GKE release schedule or the timing differences between release schedule visibility and automatic upgrade notifications
Includes comprehensive monitoring validation items like metrics collection, logging pipeline, alerting system, and extended monitoring checks
